name: Ace of Swords
short_upright: A sudden cut through fog — clarity arrives sharp and decisive. Truth, breakthrough insight, or the mental edge to name what's really going on.
short_reversed: The blade dulled or turned the wrong way. Confusion, half-truths, or clear thought weaponized into harm rather than understanding.

name: Two of Swords
short_upright: A blindfolded standoff where neither side can move. You're holding two truths in tension, postponing a decision you sense you'll eventually have to make.
short_reversed: The blindfold slips and the deadlock starts to give. New information surfaces, or the avoidance finally cracks under its own weight.

name: Three of Swords
short_upright: A clean, painful piercing of the heart. Grief, betrayal, or a truth that hurts but needed to be felt rather than buried.
short_reversed: The blades begin to withdraw and the wound starts to close. Forgiveness, healing, or the slow release of a sorrow you've carried too long.

name: Four of Swords
short_upright: A deliberate stepping back to recover and integrate. Quiet, rest, and the kind of stillness that lets the mind heal before the next round.
short_reversed: Either you can't sit still or you've sat too long. Restless exhaustion, lingering burnout, or a retreat that has slipped into avoidance.

name: Five of Swords
short_upright: A win that costs more than it's worth. Conflict driven by ego, or the bitter aftertaste of being right at someone else's expense.
short_reversed: The fight loses its grip and pride begins to soften. An opening for reconciliation, or the choice to set down a grudge you've outgrown.

name: Six of Swords
short_upright: A quiet crossing from troubled waters toward something steadier. The grief isn't gone, but you're moving — and the direction is finally forward.
short_reversed: The boat won't leave the shore, or the same currents keep pulling you back. Unfinished business and old patterns make the transition harder than it should be.

name: Seven of Swords
short_upright: Slipping away with what you came for, by cleverness rather than force. Strategy, sleight of hand, or someone working an angle that hasn't been seen yet.
short_reversed: The hidden moves come into the light. A confession, an exposure, or the moment you stop lying to yourself about what's really going on.

name: Eight of Swords
short_upright: Bound and blindfolded by a cage that's mostly mental. The trap feels total, but the swords leave a clear path out the moment you turn your head.
short_reversed: The blindfold drops and the bindings loosen. Recognizing your own role in the trap is exactly what frees you to walk away from it.

name: Nine of Swords
short_upright: Sitting up in the dark with the same thoughts circling. Anxiety and dread amplified by night and silence, often worse in the mind than in the world.
short_reversed: The grip of the worst-case story begins to loosen. Daylight returns, fears get named out loud, and the catastrophe shrinks back to manageable size.

name: Ten of Swords
short_upright: The full and final ending — there's nothing left to argue with. As bleak as it looks, the worst has already happened, and dawn is implied behind the figure.
short_reversed: Beginning to lift up off the ground, slowly. Either healing is underway, or you're still resisting an ending that has already finished itself.

name: Page of Swords
short_upright: A quick, watchful mind eager to test ideas against the world. Curious, blunt, and a little restless — the energy of learning out loud.
short_reversed: The same sharp tongue without the discipline behind it. Gossip, scattered focus, or clever talk that never quite turns into action.

name: Knight of Swords
short_upright: Full gallop toward the goal with sword raised. Bold, decisive, and impatient with anything that slows the charge — momentum as a way of being.
short_reversed: The same speed without steering. Recklessness, burnout, or words and decisions delivered too fast to land well.

name: Queen of Swords
short_upright: Perceptive and unsentimental, she sees clearly because she's been through it. Direct truth offered without cruelty, and boundaries held without apology.
short_reversed: Discernment hardened into coldness. Past hurts curdling into bitterness, judgment without warmth, or cutting people off rather than letting them in.

name: King of Swords
short_upright: The mind in command of itself — disciplined thought wielded with integrity. Sound judgment, clear principles, and the authority that comes from genuine understanding.
short_reversed: Intellect cut loose from conscience. Cold logic, rigid dogma, or using a sharp mind to dominate and manipulate rather than to clarify.
